name: iptables
title: iptables
summary: administration tools for packet filtering and NAT
description:
administration tools for packet filtering and NAT
The iptables/xtables framework has been replaced by nftables. You should
consider migrating now.
.
iptables is the userspace command line program used to configure
the Linux packet filtering and NAT ruleset. It is targeted towards systems
and networks administrators.
.
This package contains several different utilities, the most important ones:
.
iptables-nft, iptables-nft-save, iptables-nft-restore (nft-based version)
.
iptables-legacy, iptables-legacy-save, iptables-legacy-restore (legacy version)
.
ip6tables-nft, ip6tables-nft-save, ip6tables-nft-restore (nft-based version)
.
ip6tables-legacy, ip6tables-legacy-save, ip6tables-legacy-restore (legacy
version)
.
arptables-nft, arptables-nft-save, arptables-nft-restore (nft-based version)
.
ebtables-nft, ebtables-nft-save, ebtables-nft-restore (nft-based version)

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use iptables-apply because administration tools for packet filtering and NAT.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use iptables-apply as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `iptables-apply [-hV] [-t timeout] [-w savefile] {[rulesfile]|-c [runcmd]}` as documented upstream for iptables-apply.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair iptables-apply with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `iptables-apply --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: iptables-apply -- a safer way to update iptables remotely
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install iptables. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `iptables-apply [-hV] [-t timeout] [-w savefile] {[rulesfile]|-c [runcmd]}`. administration tools for packet filtering and NAT.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: administration tools for packet filtering and NAT.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: iptables-apply -- a safer way to update iptables remotely.
